description: Hold an election: candidates campaign, then the field decides by secret ballot — the vote IS the verdict, not a judge's opinion.

Staff `candidate` with 2 or more models — each makes its case and, over rounds, responds to rivals. Optionally add one `incumbent` (a candidate defending their record) and an `electorate` of silent voters who read every round but never campaign. After the final round the candidates AND the electorate cast one anonymous ballot for the strongest position — candidates may back their own, as in a real election — and a neutral `returning_officer` certifies the tally WITHOUT overruling it. Unlike battle_royale or jury, no authority picks the winner — the voters do. Use rounds >= 2 so campaigns can react.
vote: Vote for the SINGLE strongest position in the field — you may vote for your own if you truly believe it is best. Pick the number from the menu below; your ballot is anonymous, so only the totals are published.

roles.description: Make your case and campaign for it. Each round, sharpen your position and answer the strongest rival arguments — you are trying to win the votes of the field, not a judge's.
You hold the seat: defend your record and why it should continue, then campaign against the challengers' alternatives. You campaign and are voted on like any candidate.
You are a silent voter: read every round closely but do NOT campaign or post. Your only act is your secret ballot at the end — weigh who made the strongest case.

Certify the election: report the anonymous vote tally and declare the winner it produced. Do NOT substitute your own judgment — if the vote tied or split, say so plainly rather than breaking it yourself.
